sentences: Previously, text-to-image generation methods <REF_068> were based on GANs <REF_069>.
<ENT_062> et al. <REF_070> introduced a transformer-based model for generating high-quality images from provided text details.
<ENT_040> networks are also applied for image synthesis <REF_071>, which is important for learning unsupervised and generative models for downstream tasks.

Feature learning with an unsupervised training procedure <REF_072> achieves state-of-the-art performance on two datasets <REF_073>, while <ENT_063> <REF_074> provides comparable performance on <REF_075>.
The iGPT image generation network <REF_072> does not include pre-training procedures similar to language modeling tasks.
However, unsupervised <ENT_015>-based networks <REF_076> consider prior knowledge as the architectural layout, attention mechanism, and regularization.
Generative adversarial networks (<ENT_064>) <REF_069> with <ENT_015>-based backbones are appealing for image synthesis <REF_077>.
<ENT_065> <REF_078> is a strong <ENT_064> network where the generator and discriminator contain transformer modules.
These transformer-based networks boost performance for scene and image generation tasks.
